class Solution:
    def isInterleave(self, s1: str, s2: str, s3: str) -> bool:
        m, n, p = len(s1), len(s2), len(s3)

        # If the combined length of s1 and s2 is not equal to the length of s3,
        # s3 cannot be an interleaving of s1 and s2.
        if m + n != p:
            return False

        # dp[i][j] will be True if s3[0...i+j-1] is an interleaving of
        # s1[0...i-1] and s2[0...j-1].
        dp = [[False] * (n + 1) for _ in range(m + 1)]

        # Base case: empty s1 and empty s2 can form an empty s3.
        dp[0][0] = True

        # Fill the first row (when s1 is empty).
        # dp[0][j] is True if s2[0...j-1] matches s3[0...j-1].
        for j in range(1, n + 1):
            if dp[0][j-1] and s2[j-1] == s3[j-1]:
                dp[0][j] = True

        # Fill the first column (when s2 is empty).
        # dp[i][0] is True if s1[0...i-1] matches s3[0...i-1].
        for i in range(1, m + 1):
            if dp[i-1][0] and s1[i-1] == s3[i-1]:
                dp[i][0] = True

        # Fill the rest of the DP table.
        for i in range(1, m + 1):
            for j in range(1, n + 1):
                # Option 1: s3[i+j-1] comes from s1[i-1].
                # This is possible if s1[i-1] matches s3[i+j-1]
                # AND s3[0...i+j-2] is an interleaving of s1[0...i-2] and s2[0...j-1].
                from_s1 = dp[i-1][j] and s1[i-1] == s3[i+j-1]

                # Option 2: s3[i+j-1] comes from s2[j-1].
                # This is possible if s2[j-1] matches s3[i+j-1]
                # AND s3[0...i+j-2] is an interleaving of s1[0...i-1] and s2[0...j-2].
                from_s2 = dp[i][j-1] and s2[j-1] == s3[i+j-1]

                dp[i][j] = from_s1 or from_s2

        # The result is whether s3 (all of it) is an interleaving of s1 (all of it) and s2 (all of it).
        return dp[m][n]
